figures.caption: TABLE I: Comparison with Bechmark Planners. Planning performance of Flow Motion Policy and benchmark planners across evaluation planning tasks. “FMP-1” denotes Flow Motion Policy without inference-time optimization (N = 1), while “FMP-100” refers to the variant with inference-time optimization using 100 planned paths (N = 100). “T” represents planning time, and “S” denotes success rate.“ ↓” indicates lower values are preferable, and “↑” indicates higher values are preferable.

Fig. 3: Best-of-N Sampling: Planning success rate and planning time of Flow Motion Policy and Neural MP [23] adaptation across the held-out planning tasks. “GMM-1” and “GMM-100” are Neural MP [23] adaptation with Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) without inference-time optimization (N = 1) and inference-time optimization (N = 100), respectively. “FMP-1” and “FMP-100” are Flow Motion Policy without inference-time optimization and inference-time optimization (N = 100), respectively.

Fig. 11: Flow Motion Policy Deployment in Real-world. The path for the given motion planning problem is visualized for each environment, illustrating the planned motion execution across subsequent frames.

TABLE II: Policy Head Architecture Ablation: Planning success rate and planning time of Flow Motion Policy and Diffusion Motion Policy with various head architectures. “DMP-1” and “DMP-100” are Diffusion Motion Policy without inferencetime optimization and inference-time optimization (N = 100), respectively. “FMP-1” and “FMP-100” are Flow Motion Policy without inference-time optimization and inference-time optimization (N = 100), respectively. “DiT” denotes DiTBlock Policy [35]. “T” represents planning time, and “S” denotes success rate. “↓” indicates lower values are preferable, and “↑” indicates higher values are preferable.

Fig. 8: Inference Flow Steps: Success rate and average planning time of Flow Motion Policy with various head architectures across a range of inference flow steps (step ∈ [5, 90]) without inference time optimization (N = 1). “DiT” denotes DiT-Block Policy [35].

Fig. 9: Inference Flow Steps: Success rate and average planning time of Flow Motion Policy with various head architectures across a range of inference flow steps (step ∈ [5, 90]) with inference time optimization (N = 100). “DiT” denotes DiT-Block Policy [35].

Fig. 10: Real-World Deployment Setup: Illustration of the deployment of Flow Motion Policy in a real-world environment. A calibrated camera (Intel RealSense D435i RGB-D) is used to capture workspace point cloud.

Fig. 2: Flow Motion Policy architecture. This framework leverages current time-step t planning information (i.e., planning scene (Pw) point cloud, robot (Pr) point cloud, robotic manipulator configuration (qt)) together with robotic manipulator goal configuration (qgoal) for end-to-end open-loop motion planning in complex environments. ht, hr, and hw are the current time-step t planning information corresponding embeddings, respectively, and hgoal is the goal configuration embeddings. “PointNet++” are set abstraction layers from PointNet++ [26]. ϵ ∼ N (0, I) is Gaussian noise, t is flow time, and x0 is denoised increment action sequence. Best-of-N Sampling operates on a batch of planned motions and utilizes a lightweight inference-time optimization process to output the first collision-free path.

Fig. 4: Policy Head Architecture Ablation: Average planning success rate and planning time of Flow Motion Policy and Diffusion Motion Policy with various head architectures across all held-out evaluation tasks. “DMP-1” and “DMP-100” are Diffusion Motion Policy without inference-time optimization (N = 1) and inference-time optimization (N = 100), respectively. “FMP-1” and “FMP-100” are Flow Motion Policy without inference-time optimization (N = 1) and inferencetime optimization (N = 100), respectively. “DiT” denotes DiT-Block Policy [35].

Fig. 5: Diffusion Timestep Ablation: Average planning success rate and planning time of Diffusion Motion Policy with various head architectures, and diffusion timesteps (k) across all held-out evaluation tasks. “DMP-1” and “DMP-100” are Diffusion Motion Policy without inference-time optimization (N = 1) and inference-time optimization (N = 100), respectively. “DiT” denotes DiT-Block Policy [35].

Fig. 6: Diffusion Motion Policy inference Time Optimization. success rate and planning time (mean ± standard deviation) of Diffusion Motion Policy with various head architectures across a range of trajectories (N ∈ [10, 200]) in inference time optimization. “DiT” denotes DiT-Block Policy [35].

Fig. 7: Flow Motion Policy inference Time Optimization. Success rate and planning time (mean ± standard deviation) of Flow Motion Policy with various head architectures across a range of trajectories (N ∈ [10, 200]) in inference time optimization. “DiT” denotes DiT-Block Policy [35].
